title: Bajan Weather Song
description: A fun song about the weather in Barbados, incorporating the Bajan spouge rhythm and simple counting.
lyrics:
[Chorus]
Sun is shining bright, [CLAP] oh so bright!
Rain is falling down, [STOMP] all around!
Wind is blowing free, [SNAP] come and see!
Bajan weather sweet, for you and me!

[Verse 1]
One sunny day, so hot and fine,
The sun it shines, like a golden line.
We go to the beach, to swim and play,
And build sandcastles, all the day! [CLAP]

[Chorus]
Sun is shining bright, [CLAP] oh so bright!
Rain is falling down, [STOMP] all around!
Wind is blowing free, [SNAP] come and see!
Bajan weather sweet, for you and me!

[Verse 2]
Two raindrops falling, pitter-patter too,
Making puddles, shiny and new.
We wear our boots, and jump and splash,
Laughing and singing, in a rainy dash! [STOMP]

[Chorus]
Sun is shining bright, [CLAP] oh so bright!
Rain is falling down, [STOMP] all around!
Wind is blowing free, [SNAP] come and see!
Bajan weather sweet, for you and me!

[Verse 3]
Three breezy winds, blowing in the trees,
Rustling leaves, with so much ease.
Kites are flying, high in the sky,
Watch them soaring, way up high! [SNAP]

[Chorus]
Sun is shining bright, [CLAP] oh so bright!
Rain is falling down, [STOMP] all around!
Wind is blowing free, [SNAP] come and see!
Bajan weather sweet, for you and me!

[Outro]
"Wuhloss!" (Oh my!)
Bajan weather, never a bore!
We love it all, and we want more!
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant cartoon illustration showing children of various ethnicities dancing on a sunny beach in Barbados. A steelpan player is in the background, along with palm trees swaying in the breeze. One child is holding a kite, another is splashing in a puddle with colorful rain boots. The sky is a mix of bright blue and fluffy white clouds, with a small rainbow visible in the distance. Include a Caribbean-style house in the background with bright colors.
educationalValue: Teaches children about different types of weather, counting from one to three, and introduces a Bajan expression.
